message.content: Find the latest Vue.js security vulnerability (CVE) in 2025 or 2026. Give me: CVE ID, affected versions, description of the flaw, and fix/patch version. Be brief.

content.text:
Title: chore(deps): update dependency minimatch to ~10.2.0 [security] · Pull Request #14460 · vuejs/core
URL: https://github.com/vuejs/core/pull/14460
Published: 2026-02-19T22:11:31.000Z
Author: renovate[bot]
Highlights:
## chore(deps): update dependency minimatch to ~10.2.0 [security]
[...]
- Author: @renovate[bot]
- Association: CONTRIBUTOR
- State: merged
- Labels: dependencies
- Source branch: renovate/npm-minimatch-vulnerability
- Target branch: main
- Mergeable: unknown
- Commits: 1
- Additions: 3
- Deletions: 19
- Changed files: 2
- Created: 2026-02-19T22:11:31Z
- Updated: 2026-02-24T03:49:31Z
- Closed: 2026-02-24T03:49:30Z
- Merged: 2026-02-24T03:49:30Z
- Merged by: @edison1105
[...]
This PR contains the following updates:
[...]
| Package | Change | Age | Confidence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| minimatch | `~10.1.2` → `~10.2.0` | [Image: age | https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/age/npm/minimatch/10.2.1?slim=true] | [Image: confidence | https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/confidence/npm/minimatch/10.1.2/10.2.1?slim=true] |
[...]
### GitHub Vulnerability Alerts
[...]
#### CVE-2026-26996
[...]
`minimatch` is vulnerable to Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) when a glob pattern contains many consecutive `*` wildcards followed by a literal character that doesn't appear in the test string. Each `*` compiles to a separate `[^/]*?` regex group, and when the match fails, V8's regex engine backtracks exponentially across all possible splits.
[...]
The time complexity is O(4^N) where N is the number of `*` characters. With N=15, a single `minimatch()` call takes ~2 seconds. With N=34, it hangs effectively forever.
[...]
When minimatch compiles a glob pattern, each `*` becomes `[^/]*?` in the generated regex. For a pattern like `***************X***`:
[...]
When the test string doesn't contain `X`, the regex engine must try every possible way to distribute the characters across all the `[^/]*?` groups before concluding no match exists. With N groups and M characters, this is O(C(N+M, N)) — exponential.
[...]
Any application that passes user-controlled strings to `minimatch()` as the pattern argument is vulnerable to DoS. This includes:
[...]
- File search/filter UIs that accept glob patterns
- `.gitignore`-style filtering with user-defined rules
- Build tools that accept glob configuration
- Any API that exposes glob matching to untrusted input
[...]
### Release Notes
[...]
**isaacs/minimatch (minimatch)**
[...]
### `v10.2.1`
[...]
### `v10.2.0`
[...]
### `v10.1.3`
[...]
**renovate[bot]** pushed commit `bfb92e0`: chore(deps): update dependency minimatch to ~10.2.0 [security]; force-pushed the branch · Feb 24, 2026 at 3:47am

Title: CVE Request: Cross-Site Scripting via isOn() bypass in Vue.js 3.x SSR (GHSA-5w45-w79q-rpqq) · Issue #7300 · github/advisory-database
URL: https://github.com/github/advisory-database/issues/7300
Published: 2026-04-04T06:30:41.000Z
Author: sgInnora
Highlights:
## CVE Request: Cross-Site Scripting via isOn() bypass in Vue.js 3.x SSR (GHSA-5w45-w79q-rpqq)
[...]
- Author: @sgInnora
- State: open
- Created: 2026-04-04T06:30:41Z
- Updated: 2026-04-10T02:57:53Z
[...]
I am requesting GitHub CNA to assign a CVE ID for a high-severity vulnerability in Vue.js. The vendor has not responded after 10 days. This is a separate vulnerability from the RCE reported in github/advisory-database#7299.
[...]
- **GHSA**: GHSA-5w45-w79q-rpqq (submitted 2026-03-31, still in triage)
- **MITRE**: Ticket #2013988 (submitted 2026-03-25) + CVE Form (submitted 2026-04-03)
- **Vendor**: security@vuejs.org — notified 2026-03-25, **zero response**
[...]
| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Product** | Vue.js (vuejs/core) — @vue/shared, @vue/server-renderer |
| **Versions** | All Vue 3.x through 3.5.30 (latest) |
| **CWE** | CWE-79 (Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation) |
| **CVSS** | 7.2 High (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N) |
| **Type** | SSR Cross-Site Scripting — Regression of CVE-2018-6341 |
[...]
The `isOn()` function in `packages/shared/src/general.ts:15` uses a case-sensitive check that only filters event attributes starting with `on` followed by an uppercase letter (e.g., `onClick`). However, HTML event attributes are case-insensitive, so `onclick`, `ONCLICK`, `oNcLiCk` all bypass the filter and render in SSR HTML output.
[...]
- `onClick` → filtered ✓
- `onclick` → **bypasses filter** ✗ (browser still executes)
- `ONCLICK` → **bypasses filter** ✗
[...]
21 out of 21 DOM event handler attributes render in
[...]
- **CVE-2018-6341** (Vue 2.x SSR v-bind XSS): The same class of vulnerability in Vue 2, fixed in v2.5.17. Vue 3's fix is incomplete — it only blocks camelCase event attributes, not lowercase/uppercase variants.
[...]
| Date | Action |
| --- | --- |
| 2026-03-25 | Reported to security@vuejs.org |
| 2026-03-25 | MITRE ticket #2013988 submitted |
| 2026-03-31 | GitHub PVR GHSA-5w45-w79q-rpqq submitted |
| 2026-04-03 | MITRE CVE Form submitted |
| 2026-04-04 | **This GitHub CNA request** |
| 2026-06-23 | 90-day public disclosure deadline |
[...]
Please assign a CVE ID for this vulnerability. This is a distinct vulnerability (XSS, different root cause and CWE) from the RCE reported in #7299. The vendor has been completely unresponsive across all channels.
[...]
> Hi GitHub Security team,
> 
> Follow-up on this CVE request submitted April 4, 2026. This is a companion vulnerability to GHSA-pw74-q32p-x99f (#7299) — an SSR XSS via isOn() regex bypass in Vue.js 3.x (CVSS 7.2).
> 
> The Vue.js security team has not responded to either vulnerability report after 16 days. Requesting CVE assignment to proceed with coordinated disclosure.
> 
> GHSA ID: GHSA-5w45-w79q-rpqq
> 
> Thank you,
> Feng Ning — Innora (innora.ai)

---

Title: CVE Request: Remote Code Execution in Vue.js 3.x SSR Runtime Compiler (GHSA-pw74-q32p-x99f) · Issue #7299 · github/advisory-database
URL: https://github.com/github/advisory-database/issues/7299
Published: 2026-04-04T06:30:17.000Z
Author: sgInnora
Highlights:
## CVE Request: Remote Code Execution in Vue.js 3.x SSR Runtime Compiler (GHSA-pw74-q32p-x99f)
[...]
- Author: @sgInnora
- State: open
- Created: 2026-04-04T06:30:17Z
- Updated: 2026-04-10T02:57:44Z
[...]
## CVE Request — Vendor Unresponsive
[...]
I am requesting GitHub CNA to assign a CVE ID for a critical vulnerability in Vue.js that the vendor has not responded to after 10 days.
[...]
- **GHSA**: GHSA-pw74-q32p-x99f (submitted 2026-03-29, still in triage)
- **MITRE**: Ticket #2014701 (submitted 2026-03-25) + CVE Form Ticket #2019581 (submitted 2026-04-03)
- **Vendor**: security@vuejs.org — notified 2026-03-25, final notice 2026-03-31, **zero response**
[...]
| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Product** | Vue.js (vuejs/core) — @vue/server-renderer, @vue/compiler-ssr, @vue/compiler-core |
| **Versions** | All Vue 3.x through 3.5.30 (latest) |
| **CWE** | CWE-94 (Improper Control of Generation of Code) |
| **CVSS** | 10.0 Critical (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H) |
| **Type** | Server-Side Template Injection → Remote Code Execution |
[...]
Vue 3's SSR runtime template compiler (`ssrCompile.ts`) executes compiled templates via `Function('require', code)(require)` without sandboxing. The `prefixIdentifiers` transform rewrites `Identifier` AST nodes but **not** `ThisExpression` nodes, allowing `this.__proto__.constructor.constructor(...)()` to achieve arbitrary code execution in Node.js.
[...]
- **CVE-2019-19919** (Handlebars.js): Identical exploit vector — prototype chain traversal to Function constructor in a template engine. Was assigned CVE.
- **CVE-2018-6341** (Vue 2 SSR): Related Vue 2 XSS vulnerability in SSR, was assigned CVE.
[...]
Any Vue 3 SSR application that compiles templates at runtime with user-controlled content is vulnerable to Remote Code Execution. This includes CMS platforms, low-code builders, and multi-tenant SaaS applications.
[...]
| Date | Action |
| --- | --- |
| 2026-03-25 | Reported to security@vuejs.org |
| 2026-03-25 | MITRE ticket #2014701 submitted |
| 2026-03-29 | GitHub PVR GHSA-pw74-q32p-x99f submitted |
| 2026-03-31 | Final notice sent to security@vuejs.org |
| 2026-04-03 | MITRE CVE Form submitted (Ticket #2019581) |
| 2026-04-04 | **This GitHub CNA request** |
| 2026-06-23 | 90-day public disclosure deadline |
[...]
Please assign a CVE ID for this vulnerability. The vendor (Vue.js / Evan You) has been completely unresponsive for 10 days across multiple channels (direct email, GitHub PVR, MITRE).
[...]
> Hi GitHub Security team,
> 
> This CVE request was submitted on April 4, 2026 (6 days ago). The underlying vulnerability — a critical RCE (CVSS 10.0) in Vue.js 3.x SSR runtime compiler — was originally reported to the Vue.js security team (security@vuejs.org) on March 25, 2026, with zero response after 16 days.
> 
> Could you kindly provide a status update on this CVE assignment request? The 90-day coordinated disclosure deadline is June 23, 2026.
> 
> The GHSA ID is GHSA-pw74-q32p-x99f. All technical details, PoC, and root cause analysis were included in the original submission.
> 
> Thank you,
> Feng Ning — Innora (innora.ai)

---

Title: CVE-2025-53892: DOM-based XSS vulnerability in vue-i18n (npm)
URL: https://www.resolvedsecurity.com/vulnerability-catalog/CVE-2025-53892
Published: N/A
Author: N/A
Highlights:
CVE-2025-53892: DOM-based XSS vulnerability in vue-i18n (npm) tag --
[...]
# CVE-2025-53892 DOM-based XSS vulnerability in vue-i18n (npm)
[...]
DOM-based XSS No known exploit
[...]
The `escapeParameterHtml: true` option in Vue I18n fails to prevent DOM-based Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) due to improper sanitization of HTML contexts when using `v-html`. This allows tag-based payloads like ` ` to execute if interpolated into a translation string rendered with `v-html`. Despite the security option, this can lead to arbitrary client-side script execution.
[...]
- >9.0.0, <9.14.5
- >11.0.0, <11.1.10
- >10.0.0, <10.0.8
[...]
11.1.10
[...]
- >9.2.0, <9.14.5
- >11.0.0, <11.1.10
- >10.0.0, <10.0.8
[...]
This vulnerability is a DOM-based XSS in Vue I18n where the`escapeParameterHtml: true` option is bypassed. While this option correctly escapes common injection points, it does not fully sanitize entire HTML attribute contexts. Specifically, when an interpolated value containing a malicious tag-based payload (e.g.,` `) is inserted into a translation string that includes minor HTML, and this string is subsequently rendered using the`v-html` directive, the payload can execute. The`escapeParameterHtml` option escapes the`onerror` attribute's content but not the HTML tag itself if part of the source string, leading to the browser parsing and executing the unescaped tag.
[...]
Successful exploitation may allow attackers to inject and execute arbitrary client-side scripts in the user's browser, leading to session hijacking, defacement, sensitive data disclosure, or redirection to malicious sites.
[...]
Exploitation of this DOM-based XSS vulnerability is of moderate complexity. An attacker needs to control the input used in a translation parameter (`payload`) and for that translation to be rendered within the application using the`v-html` directive. No specific authentication is required beyond what is needed to input or view the affected content. It is a client-side vulnerability, typically triggered remotely when a user views a crafted page. The primary risk factor is applications that allow user-controlled input to be used in Vue I18n translations rendered with`v-html`, even with`escapeParameterHtml: true` enabled.
[...]
| PoC Author | Link | Commentary |
| --- | --- | --- |
| No known exploits |

# CVE-2025-5897: @vue/cli-plugin-pwa Regular Expression Denial of Service vulnerability
[...]
June 9, 2025
[...]
A vulnerability was found in vuejs vue-cli up to 5.0.8. It has been rated as problematic. This issue affects the function HtmlPwaPlugin of the file packages/@vue/cli-plugin-pwa/lib/HtmlPwaPlugin.js of the component Markdown Code Handler. The manipulation leads to inefficient regular expression complexity. The attack may be initiated remotely.

Title: NVD - CVE-2025-27597
URL: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-27597
Published: N/A
Author: N/A
Highlights:
NVD - CVE-2025-27597
[...]
## CVE-2025-27597 Detail
[...]
Vue I18n is the internationalization plugin for Vue.js. @intlify/message-resolver and @intlify/vue-i18n-core are vulnerable to Prototype Pollution through the entry function: handleFlatJson. An attacker can supply a payload with Object.prototype setter to introduce or modify properties within the global prototype chain, causing denial of service (DoS) a the minimum consequence. Moreover, the consequences of this vulnerability can escalate to other injection-based attacks, depending on how the library integrates within the application. For instance, if the polluted property propagates to sensitive Node.js APIs (e.g., exec, eval), it could enable an attacker to execute arbitrary commands within the application's context.

CVE Dictionary Entry: CVE-2025-27597 NVD Published Date: 03/07/2025 NVD Last Modified: 04/14/2026 Source: GitHub, Inc.

**CVE-2025-5897**

**Affected versions:** Vue CLI up to 5.0.8

**Flaw:** Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) in HtmlPwaPlugin. Inefficient regex complexity in the Markdown Code Handler causes exponential backtracking when processing certain input patterns, leading to CPU exhaustion and DoS.

**Fix/Patch:** No patch released yet (vulnerability reported June 9, 2025). Update to version 5.0.9 or higher when available.

https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-5897
